title: Callista thyrsodes
taxonomy: Callista thyrsodes is classified as a species of saltwater clams, belonging to the family Veneridae. It is a type of marine bivalve mollusk, known for its distinctive shell shape and size. The genus Callista is part of the larger family Veneridae, which includes over 600 species of saltwater clams. These mollusks are typically found in tropical and subtropical regions, including coral reefs and coastal areas. They play an important role in marine ecosystems as both predators and prey.
